auto_approve.mechanism: Gemini CLI does not document pre-configured auto-approval for specific MCP tools

enterprise_management.mechanism: Gemini CLI does not document organization-level MCP management

env_var_expansion.mechanism: env_variable_expansion: Gemini CLI MCP configuration supports environment variable expansion for secrets and paths

marketplace.mechanism: Gemini CLI does not have an in-IDE MCP marketplace

oauth_support.mechanism: Gemini CLI MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote servers

resource_referencing.mechanism: resource_referencing: Gemini CLI supports accessing MCP resources in addition to tools

tool_filtering.mechanism: tool_filtering: Gemini CLI supports per-server tool filtering to control which MCP tools are exposed to the agent

transport_types.mechanism: transport_types: Gemini CLI documents supported MCP transport protocols including stdio and HTTP

providerExtensions.name: Three transport mechanisms
providerExtensions.summary: Supports stdio, SSE, and Streamable HTTP transports — set explicitly via the type field or inferred from the connection property used.

providerExtensions.name: Per-server tool allowlist and blocklist
providerExtensions.summary: Per-server includeTools and excludeTools arrays filter which MCP tools are exposed; excludeTools takes precedence on overlap.

providerExtensions.name: Per-server trust flag
providerExtensions.summary: Boolean trust field on a server config bypasses all tool call confirmations for that server when set to true.

providerExtensions.name: Global MCP allowed/excluded lists
providerExtensions.summary: Top-level mcp.allowed and mcp.excluded arrays in settings.json globally allowlist or blocklist server names before per-server config.

providerExtensions.name: Environment variable expansion in env blocks
providerExtensions.summary: Automatically expands $VAR, ${VAR}, and %VAR% in MCP server env blocks so secrets can be referenced from the shell environment.

providerExtensions.name: CLI commands for MCP server management
providerExtensions.summary: gemini mcp subcommands and /mcp slash commands add, remove, list, enable, and disable servers without editing settings.json.

providerExtensions.name: MCP resource referencing via @ syntax
providerExtensions.summary: MCP server resources are exposed in the @ completion menu and inlined into prompts via @server://resource/path references.
